{"query":"Coppola Variety libel lawsuit ruling outcome 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1493,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/variety-asks-appeals-court-to-can-coppolas-defamation-suit-over-megalopolis-story/","title":"Variety asks appeals court to can Coppola's defamation suit over 'Megalopolis' story | Courthouse News Service","content":"LOS ANGELES (CN) — Hollywood magazine Variety asked the California Courts of Appeal on Wednesday to overrule the trial court and dismiss a defamation lawsuit brought by acclaimed film director Francis Ford Coppola over a news story about his 2024 movie, “Megalopolis.”
The 87-year-old Coppola, who directed the first two Godfather movies and Apocalypse Now, is believed to have spent around $120 million to finance his latest movie, which made back less than $15 million at the box office — a disastrous commercial failure.
Variety filed an anti-SLAPP motion to dismiss the lawsuit on First Amendment grounds, often a winning tactic for news publications.
He added that certain sentences not cited in the complaint “could be found to be defamatory.” For example, Variety quoted an anonymous source who said, “after multiple takes, Coppola got on a microphone and announced in earshot of everyone in the room, ‘Sorry, if I come up to you and kiss you.
Variety appealed the ruling."},{"url":"https://www.sfgate.com/sf-culture/article/francis-ford-coppola-lawsuit-22306547.php","title":"Francis Ford Coppola's 'Megalopolis' lawsuit against Variety drags on","content":"The lawsuit, which was originally filed in 2024, takes issue with assertions in a Variety article that followed a report by the Guardian(which has not been sued) about alleged impropriety on set.
Variety’s previous motion to dismiss the lawsuit on First Amendment grounds was denied in February 2025 by a Superior Court judge in Los Angeles because the videos did not show “conclusive evidence” of “inappropriate behavior,” as the Hollywood Reporter reported at the time, and the accusations that it made women uncomfortable could only be determined through witness statements.
Variety’s lawyer stated that it was a subjective opinion and thus protected, according to the outlet.
The winners and finalists of Best of Bay Area 2026 have been revealed."},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/variety-francis-ford-coppola-defamation-lawsuit-megalopolis-misconduct-1236148112/","title":"Variety Loses Bid to Dismiss Francis Ford Coppola Defamation Lawsuit","content":"He later sued for defamation while denying claims of sexual harassment from one of the movie’s extras, who filed a lawsuit against the director over unwanted kissing and touching."},{"url":"https://deadline.com/2024/09/francis-ford-coppola-sues-variety-megalopolis-set-behavior-1236085693/","title":"Francis Ford Coppola Sues Variety For $15M Over Megalopolis Article","content":"The 85-year-old filmmaker is seeking $15 million and further punitive and exemplary damages from defendants Variety Media LLC and journalists Brent Lang and Tatiana Siegel in the jury trial seeking complaint filed Tuesday in LA Superior Court.
“While we will not comment on active litigation, we stand by our reporters,” a Variety spokesperson told Deadline of Coppola’s suit against the publication, Siegel and Lang."},{"url":"https://globalnews.ca/news/10752924/francis-ford-coppola-lawsuit-variety-megalopolis/","title":"Francis Ford Coppola sues Variety for $15M over ‘Megalopolis’ story - National | Globalnews.ca","content":"Posted September 12, 2024 5:20 pm
Francis Ford Coppola has filed a whopping libel lawsuit against Variety magazine and two of the publication’s executive editors, after a story ran in July alleging that the director behaved inappropriately on the set of Megalopolis, his latest film set to hit theatres.
In scathing court documents, filed with the Los Angeles Superior Court Tuesday and obtained by Deadline, Coppola’s lawyers said in part that he “believes that Defendants’ libel.… was made with knowledge of falsity or with reckless disregard of the truth.”"}]}

The director is seeking $15 million in damages from Variety Media LLC and the journalists who wrote the piece, Brent Lang and Tatiana Siegel, his lawyers said in a September 11 filing. The *Variety* story included two videos of Coppola, which the authors stated showed the “director trying to kiss young female extras.” Coppola’s suit requests a jury trial and notes that anybody on set who took the videos had signed an NDA. “The signatories to the NDA agreed to hold all such information in the strictest confidence and assure that the confidential information not be published,” the suit says. “In the world of motion pictures, Plaintiff Francis Ford Coppola (‘Coppola’) is a creative genius.
